summary: Implement M4 automatic engagement resolution and siege choice loop
acceptance_criteria: When campaign forces meet an opposing force or fortified target, sim-core resolves a minimal automatic engagement using explicit deterministic inputs, stable tie-breakers, and auditable outcome reasons.

Siege choices cover the M4 vertical slice only: invest/blockade, assault, continue, accept surrender when conditions are met, lift siege, or withdraw; all are submitted through GameCommand paths shared by player and AI.
Surrender conditions and siege progress use explicit state fields and reason codes tied to supply, force, fortification, time, and defender estimates without hidden UI formulas.
Casualties, supply loss, credit/reputation hooks, and campaign status updates are emitted as serializable events for later postwar handoff and validation.
The task explicitly does not implement manual node battle, tactical battle UI, 3D battle, complex naval battle, full diplomacy, peace treaties, or multiplayer/server behavior.
required_tests: Scenario tests cover automatic field engagement, assault, blockade/continue, surrender, lift siege, and supply-driven siege failure.
Property/invariant tests cover deterministic resolution order, no negative force/supply counts, explicit casualty/loss accounting, and shared player/AI command paths.
node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-AUTO-ENGAGEMENT-SIEGE-001
notes: This is automatic resolution for the M4 vertical slice, not a commitment to manual node battles; H-007 remains a later decision.
Keep siege choices command/query based and deterministic; do not hide settlement or diplomacy logic in this task.

Explicit exclusions for M4 remain: manual node battle, complex naval warfare, full diplomacy, 3D battle, multiplayer/server, telemetry, arbitrary-code mods, and frozen product or architecture changes.
Promoted to READY after M4-MARCH-SUPPLY-STATE-001 entered origin/main at 2ab85022eaa89e5ab3b4b1f421fe422beb4b38ff and was CLOSED.
